author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
śayana⋅mandire, gaurāṅge sundara,
uṭhilā rajanī⋅śeṣe
mane dṛḍha āśa, kariba sannyāsa,
ghucāba e saba veśe

aichana bhāviyā, mandira tejīyā,
āilā suradhunī⋅tīre
dui kara juḍi, namaskāra kari,
paraśa karilā nīre

gaṅgā parihari, navadvīpa chāḍi,
kāñcana nagara⋅pathe
karilā gamana, śuni sarvajana,
vajara paḍila māthe

pāṣāṇa samāna, hṛdaya kaṭhina,
seho śuni gali jāya
paśu⋅pākhī jhure, galaye pāthare,
e dāsa locana gāya
en_translation:
In His sleeping chamber, the beautiful Lord Gaurāṅga rose at the end of the night. In His mind, He had a firm desire: "I will accept the renounced order of life and give up all these worldly garments."

Thinking thus, He left His home and came to the bank of the River Ganges. Joining His two hands in prayer, He offered His obeisances and touched the water.

Leaving the Ganges and departing from His birthplace, Navadvīpa, He proceeded on the path to the town of Kāñcana-nagara. When the people heard this, it was as if a thunderbolt had fallen upon their heads.

Even hearts as hard as stone would melt upon hearing this news. Animals and birds weep and even stones melt—so sings this servant, Locana Dāsa.
